draft.title: ai workspace
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
ai workspace

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI tools prefer dark interfaces to reduce eye strain during long coding sessions
facade.label: Dark workspace
facade.acceptImplies: Probe darker color schemes, night mode, high contrast interfaces
facade.rejectImplies: Explore bright, light, or colorful backgrounds; natural light aesthetic

evidence.hypothesis: Users building AI tools prefer dark interfaces to reduce eye strain during long coding sessions
evidence.implication: Probe darker color schemes, night mode, high contrast interfaces

facade.hypothesis: Users want key metrics visible at a glance over detailed breakdowns
facade.label: Dashboard with big numbers
facade.acceptImplies: Preference for scannable, high-level summaries; KPI-first layout; large typography for important values
facade.rejectImplies: Preference for rich context; users need to understand the full picture; skeptical of oversimplification in data presentation

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer a conversational, relaxed voice for an AI workspace—one that feels like collaborating with a friendly colleague rather than a corporate system.
facade.label: Casual chat tone
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into conversational language, informal punctuation, encouraging tone, personality-forward copy throughout the interface.
facade.rejectImplies: Pivot toward formal, structured language; maintain professional distance; use neutral, corporate phrasing; prioritize clarity over warmth.

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer direct manipulation (dragging elements) over menu-based reordering in an AI workspace, creating a sense of agency and fluid control.
facade.axisTargeted: Motion and Behavior — gesture-driven interaction vs. menu-driven interaction
facade.label: Drag to rearrange
facade.acceptImplies: The workspace should prioritize drag-and-drop, direct manipulation, and tangible object movement. Gestures feel more natural than modal dialogs.
facade.rejectImplies: The workspace should use instant, non-animated reordering (buttons, menus, keyboard shortcuts). Users prioritize speed and precision over the feeling of moving things.

facade.hypothesis: Users of AI workspaces prefer a two-level navigation structure where the primary organization uses a persistent sidebar, but secondary navigation within each section uses tabs rather than nested menus or separate views.
facade.label: Sidebar with tabs inside
facade.acceptImplies: The AI workspace should emphasize compound navigation patterns that combine persistent structure (sidebar) with flexible organization (tabs), suggesting users need both stable context and quick switching between related views.
facade.rejectImplies: Users may prefer flatter navigation (single sidebar with direct links) or fully modal/page-based organization, suggesting either simpler navigation or more complete context isolation between workspace modes.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal, clean interfaces vs. rich visual detail and color in their workspace
Whether user wants tools always visible vs. hidden/revealed on demand, and preference for sidebar vs. top navigation
Whether user responds to encouraging, conversational guidance vs. neutral, professional tone from the system
Whether user wants a calm, distraction-free environment vs. energetic, stimulating workspace atmosphere
Whether user prefers dense information displays with everything accessible vs. progressive disclosure that reveals details gradually
Whether user wants instant, snappy responses vs. smooth, animated transitions that feel deliberate and considered

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal, clean interfaces vs. rich visual detail and color in their workspace
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants tools always visible vs. hidden/revealed on demand, and preference for sidebar vs. top navigation
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to encouraging, conversational guidance vs. neutral, professional tone from the system
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants a calm, distraction-free environment vs. energetic, stimulating workspace atmosphere
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers dense information displays with everything accessible vs. progressive disclosure that reveals details gradually
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant, snappy responses vs. smooth, animated transitions that feel deliberate and considered
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces may prefer warm, diffused illumination over harsh contrast to reduce cognitive strain during long focus sessions.
facade.label: Soft shadow lighting
facade.acceptImplies: Explores calming, low-contrast visual environments that feel less like clinical tech and more like thoughtful sanctuary.
facade.rejectImplies: User gravitates toward crisp contrast, bright backgrounds, or clinical clarity—suggesting a cooler, more energetic aesthetic preference for their workspace.

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI tools prefer uncluttered interfaces with plenty of whitespace to reduce cognitive load and highlight functional elements
facade.label: Minimalist layout
facade.acceptImplies: Explore sparse grids, generous padding, single-column layouts, hidden controls that appear on hover
facade.rejectImplies: Dense layouts may be acceptable; users might value information density or compact dashboards over breathing room

draft.title: AI Workspace Hub
draft.summary: A focused workspace interface for managing AI tasks, projects, and collaborative workflows. Clean, minimal design with warm neutrals and intuitive navigation.
